matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 7 > Dinner > DIY Taco Night — Maple-Kissed Skillet

node.name: DIY Taco Night — Maple-Kissed Skillet
node.short_description: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.
node.meal_suggestion: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
node.nutrition_note: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 7 > Dinner > DIY Taco Night — Garlic-Ginger Skillet

node.name: DIY Taco Night — Garlic-Ginger Skillet
node.short_description: Lean beef tacos on corn tortillas with fresh veggies and cheddar—balanced, customizable DIY taco night.
node.meal_suggestion: Assemble 2 warm corn tortillas and fill with 3–4 oz lean ground beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, a tablespoon of salsa, and a light sprinkle of cheddar. Optional: top with avocado for healthy fats.
node.nutrition_note: Protein-rich lean beef with fiber from lettuce, tomato, salsa and corn tortillas; calcium from cheddar. Monitor sodium from salsa and cheese. For a lighter option, use 2 tortillas, 3–4 oz beef, and low-sodium salsa or reduced-fat cheddar.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 5 > Dinner > Stuffed Bell Peppers — Garlic-Ginger Bake

node.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Garlic-Ginger Bake
node.short_description: Baked stuffed bell peppers filled with ground turkey, rice, tomato sauce, and cheddar.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve 1–2 stuffed peppers per person with a green salad or steamed vegetables; for lower calories, use brown rice and reduced-fat cheddar; can be made ahead and reheated.
node.nutrition_note: Lean turkey provides protein; rice gives carbs; peppers add fiber and vitamins; cheddar supplies calcium and fat. Tomato sauce adds lycopene; sodium varies by sauce—use low-sodium options to reduce sodium.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 3 > Dinner > Turkey Meatballs & Pasta — Berry Boost Combo

node.name: Turkey Meatballs & Pasta — Berry Boost Combo
node.short_description: Turkey meatballs with whole-wheat pasta in marinara, topped with parmesan; Berry Boost indicates a berry-based fruit side for added antioxidants.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve with a side of mixed berries or a small berry compote to fit the Berry Boost concept; add steamed greens for extra fiber and micronutrients.
node.nutrition_note: Lean ground turkey provides high-quality protein; whole-wheat pasta adds fiber and complex carbs; marinara delivers tomatoes and flavor; parmesan contributes calcium. The Berry Boost name suggests pairing with a berry fruit side for extra antioxidants and vitamins.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 3 > Dinner > Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Garden Veg Style

node.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Garden Veg Style
node.short_description: Sheet-pan pork dinner featuring pork tenderloin, potatoes, green beans, and olive oil.
node.meal_suggestion: Roast pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans on a sheet pan, drizzled with olive oil and seasoned with garlic and herbs for a simple, nutritious dinner.
node.nutrition_note: Lean pork provides high-quality protein; potatoes supply complex carbs and potassium; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil contributes heart-healthy fats. For a balanced portion, aim for about 4–6 oz pork with 1–2 cups vegetables and a moderate potato serving per person.
